notes: Plaintiff opening work notes for arb-ex02-20260629181435. Opportunity openings:plaintiff, final action record_opening_statement only; evidence submission barred. Record inspected with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Evidence: ev_74d969c640f7_market-question states market title, strike Ronaldo, challenged No, requested Yes, and core issue whether 'during the FIFA World Cup Draw' includes public remarks at venue immediately before formal program or only formal on-stage program. ev_7de1d3f2c404_formal-draw-program says draft complaint states formal draw-program transcript does not appear to contain 'Ronaldo' and caveats it is not the full formal-program transcript. ev_af2671376951_pre-event-remarks says draft complaint attributes to Trump public remarks at venue immediately before formal program: 'I got to meet Ronaldo the other day.' and 'Ronaldo is great.' It caveats it is not the full upstream transcript. Native investigation: searched web for exact market/title and quoted lines. Found agentcourt examples page and GitHub ex02 source packet README; README says example was normalized from a draft complaint, did not fetch American Presidency Project transcript, Roll Call/Factba.se transcript, Kalshi page, or Bernard Bulletin post, and local files are a starting point not substitute for primary-source record. Search snippets also found Bernard Bulletin lead saying Trump said the strike to press on red carpet prior to programme and it was livestreamed, result N; only a lead and not record evidence. Potential later source targets: market page/rules/archive, pre-event press video/transcript with timestamp, formal program video/transcript, APP/Roll Call/Factba.se, Bernard Bulletin as secondary context. Theory: plaintiff can frame dispute as market-boundary interpretation; if event coverage is not limited to formal on-stage transcript, immediate venue remarks before formal program may count. Must acknowledge packet provenance limitations and formal transcript absence as defense point. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant opening work notes. Opportunity openings:defendant; allowed final action record_opening_statement only, no evidence admission. Inspected get_case and list_evidence: only three case-packet evidence items plus plaintiff opening; no submitted outside evidence. Read exact bytes and metadata for all packet files. ev_74d969c640f7_market-question: market title, strike Ronaldo, challenged No, requested Yes, core dispute whether 'during the FIFA World Cup Draw' includes public remarks at venue immediately before formal draw program began or only formal on-stage program. ev_7de1d3f2c404_formal-draw-program: draft complaint states formal draw-program transcript does not appear to contain 'Ronaldo'; file does not claim to be full formal-program transcript. ev_af2671376951_pre-event-remarks: draft complaint attributes two Ronaldo lines to Trump public remarks at venue immediately before formal program; file does not claim to be full upstream transcript. Source map: decisive elements are governing market rules/window, official/primary video or transcript scope, timestamped source for alleged pre-event remarks, and whether formal program excludes word. Web investigation as leads only: queries for market title/Ronaldo and exact alleged quote; found Kalshi market page likely event KXTRUMPMENTIONB-25DEC06 with visible rule language on page for another strike suggesting video of FIFA World Cup Draw primary and transcripts fallback; found Bernard Bulletin dispute compendium noting this Ronaldo dispute as red-carpet pre-program livestream, rules Version/Timeframe, result N. These are not admitted evidence and opening will not cite them as proof. Durable notes saved to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Opening decision: emphasize plaintiff burden and that current record only proves a dispute and draft-complaint attributions, not the full rules or upstream source. Defense theory: unless later evidence shows the market window includes pre-event red-carpet remarks, the challenged No is consistent with the admitted formal-program packet and the phrase 'immediately before' the formal program.

Plaintiff merits notes. Opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Record re-scanned with get_case/list_evidence/stat. Original packet unchanged; defendant opening added but no defendant evidence. Submitted 3 evidence items: ev_7bad2bf5c23f_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-7bad2bf5c23f (AgentCourt ex02 README provenance: packet derived from draft complaint, no downloaded source archives; APP, Roll Call/Factba.se, Kalshi page, Bernard Bulletin not fetched when example built); ev_d226db7db459_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-d226db7db459 (official White House gallery: Trump spoke to media after arrival to Kennedy Center on Dec. 5, 2025 to attend FIFA World Cup drawing; later captions show participation in drawing; does not prove word Ronaldo); ev_f538e54a608d_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-f538e54a608d (Bernard Bulletin secondary ledger: 12-5-25 Trump World Cup Draw, strike Ronaldo, evidence described as Trump said strike to press on red carpet before programme, livestreamed; rules Version/Timeframe; result N; last trade Y $.10; adjacent Infantino entry counted term in prerecorded clip during event when no exclusion). Searches: exact phrase searches for 'I got to meet Ronaldo the other day' and 'Ronaldo is great' did not find primary transcript in quick search; GitHub API tree grep found no current scratch/ronaldo.md path; opened README, Bernard, White House gallery/video, AP. Click on Bernard Kalshi link did not yield usable capture before filing. Weight: no primary market rules/transcript/video yet; provenance weakens source-fact certainty. But current preponderance favors Yes because case-packet says strike was attributed to immediate pre-event venue remarks; official WH confirms media remarks after arrival to attend drawing; secondary ledger specifically corroborates red-carpet/livestream/timeframe dispute; no admitted rule confines market to formal on-stage transcript. Journal updated /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant merits work notes. Opportunity arguments:defendant; allowed submit_evidence and submit_argument. Re-inspected get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff submitted README provenance (PX-4), White House gallery sequence (PX-5), Bernard Bulletin secondary ledger (PX-6), and a search ledger. Analysis: PX-5 supports venue/event connection but not Ronaldo or rule coverage; PX-6 is secondary and itself says result N and rules Version/Timeframe; PX-4 reduces weight of original packet. Native investigation: official Kalshi API series endpoint /series/KXTRUMPMENTIONB returned Trump Mention B, category Mentions, MENTION contract terms/product certification URLs, settlement-source list including ABC/Fox/MSNBC/WSJ/Semafor/The Information/CBS/Axios/CNN/Reuters/NYT/WaPo/AP/Politico. Kalshi public event page kxtrumpmentionb-25dec06 rendered event and sibling Canada strike: rules say resolves Yes if Trump says Canada as part of FIFA World Cup Draw; video of FIFA World Cup Draw primary; transcripts of FIFA World Cup Draw fallback by listed publications; exact phrase/plural/possessive; event must be opened to press for live televised/streamed event. Limitation: public page did not render Ronaldo strike directly; guessed API ticker KXTRUMPMENTIONB-25DEC06-RONA returned not_found; broader API paging did not locate Dec 2025 Ronaldo market. Transcript searches: Roll Call formal draw transcript 'Donald Trump Attends the FIFA World Cup Drawing in Washington' find Ronaldo returned no match; Rev formal transcript find Cristiano returned no match. Press-gaggle sources: Roll Call and Senate Democratic Caucus transcript show Ronaldo remarks, but both contain note that Trump walked red carpet for 18 minutes before the FIFA event; press pool engaged about three minutes; video/audio pieced together from TV Azteca, Telemundo, SBT Media, El Destape, among others; portions inaudible. Submitted defense evidence: ev_e32c0549826b Kalshi official source/rule structure; ev_33acff7297bc formal transcript no Ronaldo; ev_daf3876d49b8 press-gaggle adverse Ronaldo/timing-source capture. Merits theory: concede word said in press gaggle; argue proposition turns on whether pre-event red-carpet remarks count, and official rule/source material plus formal transcript absence make No more likely. Durable notes saved to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff rebuttal notes. Opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Reviewed defense evidence with stat/read. ev_e32c0549826b is Kalshi official rule/source capture for same event/sibling strike, not exact Ronaldo strike; key language says resolves Yes if Trump says strike 'as part of FIFA World Cup Draw,' video primarily used, transcript fallback, and if event not opened to press for live televised or streamed event resolves No. This press/live-stream condition helps plaintiff; capture limitation says direct Ronaldo ticker not found. ev_33acff7297bc formal Roll Call/Factbase transcript found no Ronaldo; confirms formal transcript absence only. ev_daf3876d49b8 press-gaggle/red-carpet transcript confirms multiple Ronaldo usages at 00:01:10 and 00:01:47; page title says Trump speaks to reporters at FIFA World Cup Drawing; Senate page title likewise says Trump speaks to reporters at FIFA World Cup Drawing; note says red carpet 18 minutes before FIFA event and compiled from multiple sources. Rebuttal target: defense converts 'before formal event' into market exclusion, but no exact Ronaldo rule or admitted exclusion does that. Defendant's own evidence cures plaintiff's primary-source word-use gap and shows the event was press/live-streamed, while formal transcript absence is not dispositive unless formal-only limitation is proven. Journal updated.

Defendant surrebuttal work notes. Re-inspected get_case/list_evidence after plaintiff rebuttal. No new evidence was submitted in rebuttal; plaintiff reused defense evidence as PX-R1/PX-R2/PX-R3 and argued that DX-3 confirms Ronaldo, DX-1 has no formal-stage-only exclusion, and open-to-press language supports including press/red-carpet material. Analysis: no new source search needed because rebuttal raises interpretation of existing evidence, not a new factual source target. Response: concede DX-3 confirms word use, but it also says Trump walked the red carpet for 18 minutes before the FIFA event, press pool engaged about three minutes, video/audio pieced together from multiple media sources, portions inaudible. Page title/location does not override source note. DX-1's open-to-press clause is a qualification condition, not an inclusion clause for all press-pool arrival footage; DX-1 points to video of the FIFA World Cup Draw and transcripts of the FIFA World Cup Draw. Plaintiff still bears burden to prove pre-event remarks count. Exact Ronaldo page remains uncaptured by both sides. Durable notes updated in /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff closing notes. Opportunity closings:plaintiff; final action deliver_closing_statement only; no evidence submission. Final record scanned with get_case/list_evidence/stat. Key record: defendant surrebuttal concedes Trump said Ronaldo in press-gaggle/red-carpet transcript but argues note 'before the FIFA event' excludes it. Defense evidence: ev_daf3876d... confirms Ronaldo usage in pages titled Trump speaks to reporters at the FIFA World Cup Drawing and captures timing/source-chain note; ev_e32c... is official Kalshi series/event/sibling rule capture, not exact Ronaldo page, includes 'as part of FIFA World Cup Draw,' primary video/fallback transcript, and press/live-stream qualification; ev_33ac... formal transcript no Ronaldo. Closing theory: acknowledge formal transcript absence and source-chain caveats; emphasize proposition is boundary question, word use now proven, event connection proven by WH/Factbase/Senate/Bernard, and no admitted evidence contains formal-stage-only exclusion. Under preponderance, defense's No relies on adding a narrowing rule not in record. Journal updated.

Defendant closing work notes. Final record/evidence scan via get_case/list_evidence showed no new evidence after defendant surrebuttal. Plaintiff closing argues word use is proven, event connection is proven, and defense lacks formal-stage-only exclusion. Closing theory: do not accept burden shift. Plaintiff's proposition affirmatively requires that pre-event remarks immediately before the formal program count as during the draw. Record proves Ronaldo in DX-3, but DX-3 expressly characterizes the source as red-carpet material before the FIFA event, press pool engaged about three minutes, video/audio pieced together from multiple media sources, portions inaudible. DX-2 formal draw transcript no Ronaldo is strongest source-class evidence. DX-1 is imperfect sibling/event rule context, but points to video/transcripts of the FIFA World Cup Draw, not every press/live venue feed. PX-5 proves venue/arrival connection only; PX-6 is secondary and records result N with Version/Timeframe. Exact Ronaldo page/raw video missing; that gap favors defendant because plaintiff seeks to overturn No by expanding the covered window. Durable notes updated in /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. File concise closing to reject proposition and preserve No.
